author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XIX

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To the end, a psalm of David himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is a well-known title; nor does the Lord Jesus Christ say what follows, but it is said of Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The heavens tell out the glory of God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The righteous Evangelists, in whom, as in the heavens, God dwelleth, set forth the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ, or the glory wherewith the Son glorified the Father upon earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the firmament showeth forth the works of His hands.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the firmament showeth forth the deeds of the Lord’s power, that now made heaven by the assurance of the Holy Ghost, which before was earth by fear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Day unto day uttereth word” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To the spiritual the Spirit giveth out the fulness of the unchangeable Wisdom of God, the Word which in the beginning is God with God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And night unto night announceth knowledge.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And to the fleshly, as to those afar off, the mortality of the flesh, by conveying faith, announceth future knowledge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There is no speech nor language, in which their voices are not heard” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In which the voices of the Evangelists have not been heard, seeing that the Gospel was preached in every tongue.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Their sound is gone out into all the earth, and their words to the ends of the world” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the sun hath He set His tabernacle.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now that He might war against the powers of temporal error, the Lord, being about to send not peace but a sword on earth, in time, or in manifestation, set so to say His military dwelling, that is, the dispensation of His incarnation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And He as a bridegroom coming forth out of His chamber” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And He, coming forth out of the Virgin’s womb, where God was united to man’s nature as a bridegroom to a bride.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Rejoiced as a giant to run His way.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Rejoiced as One exceeding strong, and surpassing all other men in power incomparable, not to inhabit, but to run His way.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, “He stood not in the way of sinners.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “His going forth is from the highest heaven” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From the Father is His going forth, not that in time, but from everlasting, whereby He was born of the Father.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And His meeting is even to the height of heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And in the fulness of the Godhead He meets even to an equality with the Father.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And there is none that may hide himself from His heat.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But whereas, “the Word was even made flesh, and dwelt in us,” assuming our mortality, He permitted no man to excuse himself from the shadow of death; for the heat of the Word penetrated even it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The law of the Lord is undefiled, converting souls” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The law of the Lord, therefore, is Himself who came to fulfil the law, not to destroy it; an undefiled law, “Who did no sin, neither was guile found in His mouth,” not oppressing souls with the yoke of bondage, but converting them to imitate Him in liberty.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The testimony of the Lord is sure, giving wisdom to babes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The testimony of the Lord is sure;” for, “no man knoweth the Father save the Son, and he to whomsoever the Son will reveal Him,” which things have been hidden from the wise and revealed to babes; for, “God resisteth the proud, but giveth grace to the humble.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The statutes of the Lord are right, rejoicing the heart” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All the statutes of the Lord are right in Him who taught not what He did not; that they who should imitate Him might rejoice in heart, in those things which they should do freely with love, not slavishly with fear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The commandment of the Lord is lucid, enlightening the eyes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The commandment of the Lord is lucid,” with no veil of carnal observances, enlightening the sight of the inner man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The fear of the Lord is chaste, enduring for ever” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The fear of the Lord;” not that distressing fear under the law, dreading exceedingly the withdrawal of temporal goods, by the love of which the soul commits fornication; but that chaste fear wherewith the Church, the more ardently she loves her Spouse, the more carefully does she take heed of offending Him, and therefore, “perfect love casteth” not “out” this “fear,” but it endureth for ever.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The judgments of the Lord are true, justified together.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The judgments of Him, who “judgeth no man, but hath committed all judgment unto the Son,” are justified in truth unchangeably.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For neither in His threatenings nor His promises doth God deceive any man, nor can any withdraw either from the ungodly His punishment, or from the godly His reward.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To be desired more than gold, and much precious stone” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whether it be “gold and stone itself much,” or “much precious,” or “much to be desired;” still, the judgments of God are to be desired more than the pomp of this world; by desire of which it is brought to pass that the judgments of God are not desired, but feared, or despised, or not believed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if any be himself gold and precious stone, that he may not be consumed by fire, but received into the treasury of God, more than himself does he desire the judgments of God, whose will he preferreth to his own.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And sweeter than honey and the honey comb.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And whether one be even now honey, who, disenthralled already from the chains of this life, is awaiting the day when he may come up to God’s feast; or whether he be yet as the honey comb, wrapped about with this life as it were with wax, not mixed and become one with it, but filling it, needing some pressure of God’s hand, not oppressing but expressing it, whereby from life temporal it may be strained out into life eternal: to such an one the judgments of God are sweeter than he himself is to himself, for that they are “sweeter than honey and the honey comb.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thy servant keepeth them” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to him who keepeth them not the day of the Lord is bitter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In keeping them there is great reward.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not in any external benefit, but in the thing itself, that God’s judgments are kept, is there great reward; great because one rejoiceth therein.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who understandeth sins?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what sort of sweetness can there be in sins, where there is no understanding?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For who can understand sins, which close the very eye, to which truth is pleasant, to which the judgments of God are desirable and sweet? yea, as darkness closes the eye, so do sins the mind, and suffer it not to see either the light, or itself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Cleanse me, O Lord, from my secret faults.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From the lusts which lie hid in me, cleanse me, O Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And from the” faults “of others preserve Thy servant” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let me not be led astray by others.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he is not a prey to the faults of others, who is cleansed from his own.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Preserve therefore from the lusts of others, not the proud man, and him who would be his own master, but, Thy servant.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If they get not the dominion over me, then shall I be undefiled.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If neither my own secret sins, nor those of others, get the dominion over me, then shall I be undefiled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there is no third source of sin, but one’s own secret sin, by which the devil fell, and another’s sin, by which man is seduced, so as by consenting to make it his own.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I shall be cleansed from the great offence.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What but pride? for there is none greater than apostasy from God, which is “the beginning of the pride of man.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And he shall indeed be undefiled, who is free from this offence also; for this is the last to them who are returning to God, which was the first as they departed from Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the words of my mouth shall be pleasing, and the meditation of my heart is always in Thy sight” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The meditation of my heart is not after the vain glory of pleasing men, for now there is pride no more, but in Thy sight alway, who regardest a pure conscience.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O Lord, my Helper, and my Redeemer” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O Lord, my Helper, in my approach to Thee; for Thou art my Redeemer, that I might set out unto Thee: lest any attributing to his own wisdom his conversion to Thee, or to his own strength his attaining to Thee, should be rather driven back by Thee, who resistest the proud; for he is not cleansed from the great offence, nor pleasing in Thy sight, who redeemest us that we may be converted, and helpest us that we may attain unto Thee.
